title: Unicode, Language, Tokenization, Fields, and Citation Offsets · Program Defense
summary: A release board compares near-miss decisions for unicode, language, tokenization, fields, and citation offsets and accepts only runnable changed-case evidence, safe abstention, explicit uncertainty, and named native-model, vector-store, provider, load, security, recovery, or production transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for reversible multilingual text pipeline. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debu…
objectives: Choose and version Unicode normalization per field while retaining original text and testing canonical and compatibility distinctions.
Detect or receive language and apply language- and script-appropriate word, subword, character, or morphological tokenization.
Separate title, heading, body, code, identifier, date, author, tenant, and access metadata with explicit weighting and filter semantics.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""rag-retrieval-augmented-generation cumulative evidence-grounded retrieval portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python models and fixed fixtures only.
Native ML packages, files, models, vector stores, provider APIs, networks, credentials,
OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production effects require authorized transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RagEvidence:
    corpus_version: str
    document_id: str
    chunk_id: str
    query_id: str
    source_span: str


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RetrievalBoundary:
    tenant_id: str
    authorized: bool
    answerable: bool
    context_budget: int


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python RAG engineering portfolio ready"

steps.title: RAG and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Rag Offset Lineage
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Rag Offset Lineage: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A quiz RAG engineering team handles case 9738 during a multilingual support escalation. Build deterministic Python evidence to normalize and tokenize multilingual fields without losing source text or citation offsets; keep every status keyboard reachable, announced, readable without color, and linked to structured source evidence; then replace one exact identifier with a synonym and compare lexical, dense, and hybrid recall. Browser code uses pure bounded models and fixed fixtures only. Native Python packages, embedding or generation models, vector databases, files, networks, credentials, provider APIs, OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Maintain mappings from normalized tokens and chunks back to exact document spans, pages, regions, and versions.

Evidence target: RAG and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Rag Offset Lineage.
steps.why: Retrieving Rag Offset Lineage through the concrete reversible multilingual text pipeline case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For RAG and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Rag Offset Lineage, keep Rag Offset Lineage connected to executable Python retrieval, grounding, evaluation, and evidence decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Maintain mappings from normalized tokens and chunks back to exact document spans, pages, regions, and versions.
